text: A rapidly evolving research area, Transformers have achieved impressive success in many NLP and CV tasks. This has not only raised public awareness of Transformers, but also sparked strong interest in the time series community. While other benefits of using Transformers have been highlighted in many works, the ability of Transformers to model long-range dependency and relationships in data has been largely responsible for the recent breakthroughs in time series modeling and analysis, which makes this area an exciting one. This survey paper aims to provide a comprehensive overview of the properties and impacts of using Transformers for time series modeling. We investigate the development of time series Transformers from two viewpoints: (i) structural view from the network perspective, where we summarize the necessary modifications and operations on original Transformers to address the characteristics of time series data; and (ii) application view from various use cases such as forecasting, anomaly detection, and classification. We carry out the robustness analysis and the model size analysis to evaluate the performances of the proposed time series Transformers, as well as the trend analysis for seasonality decomposition. The insights from the analyses are discussed, and some possible future research ways are given to guide the community.
